author: joelarmstrong
text:
At medium-large scale, especially if you launch a ton of containers at once, you can end up running up against the containerd launch timeout. This happened especially often previous to the 1.12 series (see docker issue #22226), but I've been getting these even after the AWS provisioner switched over to a CoreOS version containing 1.12.3. (My appliance contains a 1.12.3 client, too). This is obviously a Docker issue, but could Toil retry retriable Docker failures like this? The symptom is that Docker exits with return code 125 (ECANCELLED), and outputs an error message to stderr:


(Incidentally, I don't think \_fixPermissions actually needs to be run if Toil is being run in the toil appliance. Maybe it could detect that it's being run as root and not bother changing any permissions?)
